Issues.Title: Documentation site is doing thousand of failing network requests....
Issues.Body:
I experienced that behaviour since some day ago, looking for reference details. While browsing the site it became stuck, no more responding. 
I had a quick view to devtools network tab and saw there were lots of requests failing and repeating quickly, until the page stop working.
Am I the only one having this problem?
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open issue "Documentation site is doing thousand of failing network requests...." (#4047)

Issues.Title: fix(form): correct .control selector edge case for last-child grouping (#4045)
Issues.Body:
This is a bugfix.

Proposed solution

Fix incorrect addon behavior in .control grouping when non-.control siblings (e.g. label) are present. Structural pseudo-classes (:last-child, :only-child, etc.) are replaced with their :of-type equivalents to avoid interference from non-control elements.

Tradeoffs

:of-type is based on element type, not class. Edge cases may still occur if other sibling elements share the same tag name.

Testing Done

Manual test with a label followed by a single .control. Verified correct addon behavior.

Changelog updated?

No.

Issue

Fixes #4045
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open PR "fix(form): correct .control selector edge case for last-child grouping (#4045)" (#4046)

Issues.Title: Statamic usage?
Issues.Body:
Hi there,
I am working on a website in Statamic, but I am getting tired of Tailwind, and having to use LLM's for everything (I know I know). I personally am not a frontend dev, but can do a little, and bootstrap is easy enough even for me. Tailwind is like drinking cactus needles.

Now, someone on discord suggested me to look at Bulma. Now I would like to know if someone has ever managed to make it work with Statamic or not. Or knows where I should look. 

Thanks in advance!
Issues.RelatedDescription: Closed issue "Statamic usage?" (#4043)

Issues.Body: **Location:** node_modules/bulma/sass/form/tools.scss There is a CSS logic issue when using the selector .control:last-child:not(:only-child). Because :last-child and :only-child are structural pseudo-classes, they evaluate all child nodes within a parent, ignoring the .control class itself. If a .control element is the last element in a container but is preceded by a non-control element (like
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open issue "Unintended behavior with .control:last-child:not(:only-child) when other sibling tags are present" (#4045)

Issues.Title: Documentation web pages not responsive
Issues.Body:
Since the whole point of Bulma is that it consists of responsive CSS, the web pages that document Bulma should be responsive. But they are not.

For example, try to read

https://bulma.io/documentation/start/installation/

on a mobile phone. You can't. The text does not wrap; it runs off the right side of the screen, and you cannot scroll it in order to read it.

I do not know whether this is an issue with the documentation page itself or an underlying flaw in the Bulma-generated CSS. But as a matter of principle it seems to me that the documentation pages should illustrate Bulma in action, in a good way.
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open issue "Documentation web pages not responsive" (#4041)

Issues.Title: [Request] Release filename change
Issues.Body: Currently the filenames for Bulma releases are `bulma-<version>.zip`, github allows linking directly to `/releases/latest/download/asset-name.zip` however, which would require a consistent name across versions. Would it be possible to have a more consistent filename for the release bundle, so it's possible to stay up-to-date without having to manually modify scripts and workflows?
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open issue "[Request] Release filename change" (#4038)

Issues.Title: Fix: use CSS custom properties in has-text-weight-* helpers for runtime theming
Issues.Body:
## Summary

- Replaces hard-coded SCSS variable values with CSS custom properties (`var(--bulma-weight-*)`) in all 6 `has-text-weight-*` helper classes
- Enables runtime font weight customization by overriding `--bulma-weight-*` CSS variables, consistent with how other Bulma components (e.g. `.title`) already reference weight values
- Fully backward compatible: `--bulma-weight-*` variables are already registered in `light.scss`, so behavior is unchanged without customization

## Motivation

The `has-text-weight-*` helper classes previously compiled to static numeric values (e.g. `font-weight: 800 !important`), while other components like `.title` use `var(--bulma-weight-extrabold)`. This inconsistency means overriding `--bulma-weight-*` CSS variables does not affect the helper classes. Relates to #3984.

## Changes

**`sass/helpers/typography.scss`**
- Added `@use "../utilities/css-variables" as cv;`
- Changed all 6 `has-text-weight-*` classes from `iv.$weight-*` to `cv.getVar("weight-*")`

**Compiled CSS output (all versions rebuilt)**
```css
/* Before */
.has-text-weight-extrabold { font-weight: 800 !important; }

/* After */
.has-text-weight-extrabold { font-weight: var(--bulma-weight-extrabold) !important; }
```

## Test plan

- [x] All 6 `has-text-weight-*` classes output `var(--bulma-weight-*)` in compiled CSS
- [x] All CSS versions rebuilt (`bulma.css`, `bulma-no-dark-mode.css`, `bulma-prefixed.css`)
- [x] `bulma-no-helpers` variants correctly exclude these classes (no change)
- [x] Overriding `:root { --bulma-weight-bold: 900; }` is reflected in `.has-text-weight-bold`
- [x] Default behavior unchanged when no CSS variables are overridden
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open PR "Fix: use CSS custom properties in has-text-weight-* helpers for runtime theming" (#4036)

Issues.Title: Support install via Composer
Issues.Body:
Add support to install Bulma via [Composer](https://getcomposer.org).

This is an  **improvement**.

Some PHP frameworks, such as [Symfony](https://symfony.com) have their own way to compile sass files, via Dart Sass, i.e. [Symfony sass-bundle](https://symfony.com/bundles/SassBundle/current/index.html).
Since those packages don't rely on npm, it could be great if Bulma is installable via Composer.

### Proposed solution

Actually, it's a very easy task:

-  Add `composer.json` file to the Bulma repository root (this PR subject).
-  Register Bulma repository on [Packagist](https://packagist.org/).
### Tradeoffs

None.

### Testing Done

No need.

### Changelog updated?

No.
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open PR "Support install via Composer" (#4034)

Issues.Title: chore: Update outdated GitHub Actions version
Issues.Body:
- This is a **new feature**.
- Proposed solution: Updates `actions/checkout` from `v4` to `v6` in `.github/workflows/main.yml`.
- Tradeoffs: A major version update may require adjustments in workflows, but the benefits include newer features and improvements.
- Testing Done: None.
- Changelog updated?: No.
Issues.RelatedDescription: Closed or merged PR "chore: Update outdated GitHub Actions version" (#4033)

Issues.Title: Add Ctrl+K / Cmd+K shortcut to navbar search
Issues.Body:
### What this does
- Adds a global `Ctrl + K` / `Cmd + K` keyboard shortcut to open the navbar search
- Displays a keyboard shortcut hint next to the search icon on desktop

### Why
This improves search discoverability and matches common UX patterns used in modern developer-focused websites.

Fixes #4031

### Implementation details
- Reuses the existing search toggle behavior
- Plain JavaScript, no new dependencies
- Desktop-only visual hint; mobile UI remains unchanged

### Testing done
- Verified locally using `jekyll serve`
- Tested keyboard shortcut and click behavior
- Confirmed no layout changes on mobile
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open PR "Add Ctrl+K / Cmd+K shortcut to navbar search" (#4032)

Issues.Title: Fix navbar-item hover styles for button elements
Issues.Body:
This is a **bugfix**.

### Proposed solution

Fixed hover, focus, and active background styles for `<button class="navbar-item">`.  
The issue was that the existing selector targeted only:
### Issue
Closes #3936

- `a.navbar-item`
- `.navbar-link`

…so buttons were missing the hover/focus/active behavior.  

The fix is **simple and safe**: add `button.navbar-item` to the selector.

```scss
a.#{iv.$class-prefix}navbar-item,
button.#{iv.$class-prefix}navbar-item,
.#{iv.$class-prefix}navbar-link {
  ...
}
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open PR "Fix navbar-item hover styles for button elements" (#4030)

Issues.Title: Request for new color tag named "error" for buttons, text etc
Issues.Body:
Currently we have multiple color tags like `is-link` , `is-success` , `is-warning`  for creating buttons and texts.

Please create a new tag `is-error` just like `is-danger`.

In programming `"success"`, `"error"`, `"warning"`, and `"info"` are most used labels.

The new `is-error` color label will be much more useful in data labeling.
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open issue "Request for new color tag named "error" for buttons, text etc" (#4027)

Issues.Title: Is the project active?
Issues.Body:
Hello!  First and foremost, thanks for the library.  Us, the artistic-impaired, appreciate it very much.

Second:  I ask if the project is currently maintained.  I fear the amount of open issues and the issues that have gone unanswered.

The reason I ask is that I was thinking about creating a Svelte component library for Bulma, since current projects haven't updated and I really liked it when I did this [live demo of my router in Sveltekit](https://wjsoftware.github.io/svelte-router-kit/).

To create a component library takes time and effort, so I figured I should first ask so as to not potentially waste said time and effort which can be focused on something else.

Thank you!
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open issue "Is the project active?" (#4025)

Issues.Title: Still maintained?
Issues.Body:
Hi @jgthms 

Thanks for the excellent CSS framework. But what's the status of this project? I see the last code change was 6 months ago yet there are 177 PRs from people who want to improve it.
Issues.RelatedDescription: Closed issue "Still maintained?" (#4024)

Issues.Title: Support dart-sass' `pkg:` importer
Issues.Body:
Hi, dart-sass gained a cool feature that allows importing packages from various package managers [by prefixing their url with `pkg:`](https://sass-lang.com/documentation/at-rules/use/#node-js-package-importer).

To properly support this for node packages, the `package.json` file needs to list its sass/scss files explicitly in `exporters` - at least the index. Currently bulma doesn't do this, so relative imports with `pkg:` don't always resolve correctly. It'd be nice if that field could be added to `package.json`.
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open issue "Support dart-sass' `pkg:` importer" (#4023)

Issues.Title: `select.is-skeleton` is inconsistent with other skeletons
Issues.Body:
Adding the `is-skeleton` class to a select element produces a result that doesn't align with the rest of the skeleton visual style ```html
``` Adding the `is-skeleton` class directly to the select element at least hides the text ```html
``` Setting the `select` element to `display: none;` helps, but the arrow still displays, and you have to give it a manual width ```html
``` EDIT: Setting the select element to disabled also shows through the skeleton, which looks weird Am I missing something? Or is this intended behaviour? In a similar vein, I would expect wrapping elements in a `div.skeleton-block` would wide them or apply their skeleton styles, but it leaves the content visible
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open issue "`select.is-skeleton` is inconsistent with other skeletons" (#4022)

Issues.Title: .is-light.is-outlined button class combination no longer working since v.1.x
Issues.Body: Prior to v1, the combination of **light** and **outlined** button classes produced a light coloured button with the addition of an outline: But since v1, this combination now knocks out the background colour to `transparent` resulting in the exact same style for `is-light is-outlined` and `is-outlined` buttons: This means any buttons with those class combinations don't really work as expected in their context anymore, so as a workaround I've implemented a little local override: ```scss .button.is-light.is-outlined { color: hsl(var(--bulma-button-h), var(--bulma-button-s), var(--bulma-button-color-l)); background-color: hsl(var(--bulma-button-h), var(--bulma-button-s), calc(var(--bulma-button-background-l) + var(--bulma-button-background-l-delta))); } ``` I can't remember if the old documentation ever documented this specific class combination or not, but regardless of whether it was an intentional design I wonder if it might still be nice for it to work in v1? Codepen links here with the examples above: [Bulma v0.9.4 .is-light.is-outlined class combination](https://codepen.io/bluefantail/pen/pvgpXBY) [Patch for Bulma v1.x .is-light.is-outlined class combination](https://codepen.io/bluefantail/pen/PwZErdO) Happy to open a PR with a change suggestion 🙂.
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open issue ".is-light.is-outlined button class combination no longer working since v.1.x" (#4020)
